_note: ORIGINAL content. Topic coverage follows the curriculum in Runestone Foundations of Python Programming, Think Python, and OpenStax Introduction to Python Programming. No prose, examples, or exercises were copied; the sources guide topic selection only.
title: Modules & Imports
summary: Split a program into files with clear jobs.
refresher: A Python file is a module. Use `import module` to keep its name visible, or `from module import name` to bring in one item. Put code that should run only when a file starts the program under `if __name__ == "__main__":`.

blocks.body: One large file becomes difficult to scan. Modules let you place related functions and classes in smaller files. A grade calculator can live in one module while the main program handles input and output.

blocks.caption: Importing the module keeps the source of `sqrt` clear. The dot says that the function comes from math.

blocks.body: Python loads a module the first time it is imported during a run. It creates the module's names and makes them available to the importing file.

blocks.caption: A wildcard import hides where names came from and can replace an existing name. Importing the module keeps that boundary visible.

blocks.title: Imported files can run code
blocks.body: Statements at the top level run when Python imports the file. Place demo code and direct user input inside a main guard so importing the module does not start them.

blocks.prompt: Which line makes the `random` module available under its own name?
blocks.why: `import random` binds the module name. Its functions are then reached with a dot, such as `random.randint(1, 6)`.

blocks.body: The main guard checks how the file was started. Its block runs during direct execution, but not when another file imports the module.

choices: When this file is run directly
Every time the file is imported
blocks.why: Python sets `__name__` to `"__main__"` for the file that starts the program. Imported modules receive their module names instead.

blocks.title: Module checklist
items: Group code by one clear purpose.
Prefer imports that show where a name came from.
Put demos and command-line input behind a main guard.
